skill.name: industrial-brutalist-ui
skill.description: Design guidance for industrial brutalist interfaces that combine Swiss typography, tactical telemetry layouts, rigid grids, and analog visual effects.
skill.summary: Build raw, data-heavy interfaces with industrial grids, tactical typography, and disciplined visual constraints.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but manual review found only Markdown design guidance and inline code examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or malicious instructions were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are inline Markdown code examples for CSS values, HTML tags, colors, and decorative symbols. They are not Ruby shell backticks and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are visible Markdown formatting around non-executable design tokens. The skill contains no script files or command invocation syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Windows SAM Database
low_findings.description: The sensitive data finding appears to match ordinary text inside a decorative framing example. No Windows SAM database path, credential dumping instruction, or registry access is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 77 only shows bracketed interface decoration text. There is no evidence of Windows security account data or related access instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are caused by design language and visual effect terminology. No hashing, encryption, password storage, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe interface aesthetics, typography, and visual degradation. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive implementation appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are triggered by words related to tactical interface styling, terminal aesthetics, and CSS layout. The skill does not instruct system inspection or host enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms are used in a design-system context, not as operational security commands. No evidence of system discovery, process listing, or network enumeration was found.

content.value_statement: Dense technical products often need stronger structure than standard web UI patterns provide. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear rules for raw industrial interface design.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection between Swiss industrial print and tactical telemetry visual modes.
Defines typography rules for macro headers, metadata, navigation, and data labels.
Specifies light and dark color palettes with strict accent color constraints.
Recommends grid structures, borders, semantic tags, and fixed mechanical layouts.
Describes CSS and SVG effects for halftone, dithering, scanlines, and surface noise.
Provides component styling direction for dense dashboards, portfolios, and editorial interfaces.
limitations: It is a style guide, not a complete UI component library.
It does not include executable code, assets, templates, or test suites.
It favors severe visual systems that may not fit consumer or accessibility-first products.
It requires human review to balance visual density with readability and usability.

use_cases.title: Design a Technical Operations Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create a dense telemetry layout with rigid compartments, monospace metadata, status indicators, and disciplined contrast.

use_cases.title: Restyle a Developer Portfolio
use_cases.description: Apply Swiss industrial typography, hard grid divisions, and restrained red accents to a personal site.

use_cases.title: Direct an Editorial Microsite
use_cases.description: Use oversized type, print-inspired layouts, and analog texture to build a raw technical publication.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Visual Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new interface concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the industrial-brutalist-ui skill to propose one visual direction for a data-heavy dashboard. Choose either Swiss Industrial Print or Tactical Telemetry.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Layout Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Using industrial-brutalist-ui, define the grid, typography scale, palette, and key sections for a technical operations dashboard.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing UI
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for design critique and revision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface against industrial-brutalist-ui. Identify mismatches, then revise the layout, color, typography, and component treatment.

prompt_templates.title: Specify Implementation Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use industrial-brutalist-ui to create implementation guidance for CSS grid, semantic HTML, typography clamping, borders, and analog texture effects.

output_examples.input: Design direction for a machine monitoring dashboard.
output_examples.output: A tactical telemetry interface with dark CRT substrate, compact monospace labels, red hazard accents, hard grid compartments, and scanline texture.

output_examples.input: Portfolio homepage concept for an industrial designer.
output_examples.output: A Swiss industrial print layout with oversized uppercase headers, off-white paper background, black structural lines, and sparse red annotations.

output_examples.input: Editorial page for a technical report.
output_examples.output: A rigid modular page with macro typography, dense metadata blocks, halftone image treatment, and visible horizontal divisions.

best_practices: Choose one visual archetype per project and keep it consistent.
Use strict grid rules, sharp corners, and visible divisions to support scanability.
Reserve accent color for alerts, critical values, and structural emphasis.
anti_patterns: Do not mix light print styling with dark terminal styling in one interface.
Do not add gradients, soft shadows, rounded cards, or decorative translucency.
Do not let texture effects reduce readability of core data or controls.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create industrial brutalist web interfaces for dashboards, portfolios, editorial pages, and telemetry-style products.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It contains written design guidance and examples, not scripts or runnable components.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it suitable for every product?
faq.answer: No. It works best for technical, data-heavy, editorial, or intentionally severe visual systems.

faq.question: Can it support accessibility goals?
faq.answer: It can guide structure and contrast, but teams must still test readability, focus states, and responsive behavior.

faq.question: Why did the security scan flag it?
faq.answer: The scanner matched Markdown backticks and design terms as risky patterns. Manual review found no executable threat.
